title: picea excelsa var. alpestris
intro: Picea excelsa var. alpestris, also known as the alpine spruce, is a subspecies of the European spruce tree (Picea abies), specifically adapted to grow in alpine regions. This coniferous evergreen tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Picea excelsa var. alpestris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and needles of the alpine spruce are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, including colds and coughs.
